content:
FINAL ENTERPRISE DATA PROTECTION, PITR, IMMUTABLE BACKUP,
DISASTER RECOVERY, AND OWNER RECOVERY CENTER

The current IVX recovery framework is a good foundation, but it is not yet
enterprise-certified.

Current known gaps:

1. Supabase PITR is not confirmed.
2. Only one visible local snapshot exists.
3. Daily recovery reports are not proven.
4. There is no owner-facing Data Recovery screen.
5. Storage/media backup is not proven.
6. Cross-account/off-site recovery is not proven.
7. Full restore drills are not proven.
8. RPO and RTO are not formally defined or tested.

Complete the full enterprise data-protection architecture now.

Do not overwrite production during testing.
Do not fabricate restore evidence.
Do not call a snapshot successful until it has been restored and validated.
Do not store all backups in the same project/account as production.

==================================================
1. DEFINE ENTERPRISE RECOVERY OBJECTIVES
==================================================

Define and enforce:

RPO_TARGET=
RTO_TARGET=
BACKUP_RETENTION=
PITR_RETENTION=
SNAPSHOT_FREQUENCY=
RESTORE_TEST_FREQUENCY=
OFFSITE_COPY_FREQUENCY=

Recommended initial IVX targets:

- Database RPO: 5 minutes or less
- Database RTO: 60 minutes or less
- Critical identity/financial RPO: near-continuous
- Storage/media RPO: 24 hours or less
- Daily snapshot retention: 35 days
- Monthly archive retention: 12 months
- Restore drill: monthly
- Full disaster-recovery drill: quarterly

Return justification and actual supported values.

==================================================
2. CONFIRM AND ENABLE SUPABASE PITR
==================================================

Owner must verify in Supabase Dashboard:

Database
→ Backups
→ Point-in-Time Recovery

Return:

SUPABASE_PROJECT=
PLAN=
PITR_AVAILABLE=
PITR_ENABLED=
RETENTION_DAYS=
EARLIEST_RESTORE_TIME=
LATEST_RESTORE_TIME=
WAL_STATUS=
OWNER_ACTION_REQUIRED=

If PITR is not available on the current plan:

- state the exact required plan/add-on;
- do not claim enterprise recovery complete;
- continue implementing independent logical/off-site backups.

PITR must allow restoration into an isolated recovery environment before any
production recovery decision.

==================================================
3. AUTOMATED DATABASE BACKUPS
==================================================

Implement multiple backup layers:

A. CONTINUOUS

- PITR/WAL where available

B. DAILY

- encrypted full logical database dump
- schema dump
- roles/policies dump
- migration history
- row-count manifest
- checksum manifest

C. WEEKLY

- complete verified export
- copied to separate owner-controlled storage

D. MONTHLY

- immutable archive
- long-term retention
- separate account or region

Backup all schemas required for recovery, including:

- public
- auth-related recoverable metadata where supported
- storage metadata
- custom schemas
- RLS policies
- functions
- triggers
- views
- extensions list
- migrations

Return:

BACKUP_JOB=
SCHEDULE=
DESTINATION=
ENCRYPTION=
RETENTION=
LAST_SUCCESS=
CHECKSUM=
RESTORE_TESTED=

==================================================
4. STORAGE AND MEDIA BACKUP
==================================================

Supabase database backups do not automatically protect the actual objects in
Storage buckets.

Audit and back up:

- property images
- property videos
- member avatars
- investor documents
- contracts
- PDFs
- KYC files
- statements
- legal documents
- chat attachments
- AI-generated media
- thumbnails
- exports

For every bucket return:

BUCKET
OBJECT_COUNT
TOTAL_BYTES
VERSIONING
BACKUP_DESTINATION
LAST_BACKUP
CHECKSUM_VERIFIED
RESTORE_TESTED
STATUS

Implement:

- object versioning where supported;
- lifecycle policies;
- deleted-object retention;
- off-site encrypted copy;
- manifest of object paths and hashes;
- restore verification.

==================================================
5. OFF-SITE AND CROSS-ACCOUNT BACKUPS
==================================================

Production and all backups must not exist only in the same Supabase project or
same cloud account.

Create owner-controlled off-site copies in:

- separate AWS S3 backup bucket/account, or
- another approved independent backup provider.

Requirements:

- separate credentials;
- least privilege;
- server-side encryption;
- object lock/immutability where supported;
- versioning;
- retention policy;
- deletion protection;
- no public access;
- cross-region copy where appropriate;
- owner-only recovery permission.

Return:

PRIMARY_BACKUP_LOCATION=
SECONDARY_BACKUP_LOCATION=
ACCOUNT_SEPARATION=
REGION_SEPARATION=
IMMUTABILITY_ENABLED=
VERSIONING_ENABLED=
DELETE_PROTECTION=
LAST_COPY_STATUS=

==================================================
6. IMMUTABLE BACKUPS
==================================================

Create backup copies that application code, Rork, normal administrators, and
compromised production credentials cannot silently delete or modify.

Implement:

- write-once retention where supported;
- separate backup role;
- MFA/owner approval for deletion;
- retention lock;
- append-only audit trail;
- backup deletion alerts.

Test:

1. normal backend attempts deletion;
2. deletion is denied;
3. unauthorized overwrite is denied;
4. owner recovery role can access restore copy;
5. audit event is generated.

==================================================
7. PRE-DEPLOYMENT AND PRE-MIGRATION SNAPSHOTS
==================================================

Before every:

- database migration;
- production deployment;
- schema modification;
- bulk import;
- member reconciliation;
- financial operation;
- role/RLS change;

automatically create:

- snapshot ID;
- row counts;
- schema checksum;
- migration version;
- GitHub SHA;
- Render deploy ID;
- timestamp;
- rollback plan.

Block high-risk deployment if the pre-deployment backup fails.

==================================================
8. DATA-LOSS GUARD
==================================================

Keep and strengthen the existing destructive-operation guard.

Protected entities must include:

- members
- profiles
- auth identities
- investors
- buyers
- JV deals
- wallets
- ledger
- treasury
- transactions
- holdings
- withdrawals
- deposits
- revenue
- fees
- properties
- documents
- KYC
- referrals
- waitlist
- registrations
- conversations
- messages
- audit logs
- variables metadata

Require for destructive operations:

1. owner JWT;
2. permission check;
3. recent reauthentication;
4. reason;
5. impact preview;
6. affected-row count;
7. backup confirmation;
8. two-person approval for critical financial/bulk deletion;
9. soft delete first;
10. delayed hard deletion;
11. audit event;
12. recovery reference.

==================================================
9. SOFT DELETE AND RETENTION
==================================================

Critical records must not be immediately hard-deleted.

Implement:

- deleted_at
- deleted_by
- deletion_reason
- recovery_deadline
- original_state
- restored_at
- restored_by

Retention examples:

- users/members: 90 days
- CRM contacts: 90 days
- financial records: never hard-delete through application
- audit logs: append-only
- chat/media: configurable legal retention
- properties/deals: archive instead of delete

Owner must be able to restore records during the retention window.

==================================================
10. BACKUP VALIDATION
==================================================

A successful upload is not proof of a usable backup.

Every backup job must validate:

- file exists;
- size > 0;
- checksum matches;
- encryption works;
- manifest exists;
- schema parses;
- row counts match expected ranges;
- critical tables are included;
- storage manifest is complete.

Alert immediately if:

- backup missing;
- row count unexpectedly drops;
- checksum changes unexpectedly;
- backup size changes abnormally;
- PITR is disabled;
- storage copy fails;
- retention policy changes.

==================================================
11. AUTOMATED RESTORE DRILLS
==================================================

Run scheduled restoration tests into an isolated environment.

Restore and verify:

- schema
- RLS
- functions
- members
- investors
- properties
- deals
- wallets
- transactions
- conversations
- messages
- storage objects

Required checks:

- application can connect;
- owner can sign in;
- row counts match;
- foreign keys valid;
- financial totals reconcile;
- storage files open;
- chat history loads;
- no production writes occur.

Return:

RESTORE_DRILL_ID
BACKUP_SOURCE
RESTORE_TARGET
START_TIME
END_TIME
RTO_ACTUAL
RPO_ACTUAL
TABLES_VERIFIED
OBJECTS_VERIFIED
FAILED_CHECKS
FINAL_STATUS

==================================================
12. DISASTER RECOVERY RUNBOOK
==================================================

Create an owner-readable runbook for:

- accidental deletion;
- bad migration;
- compromised credentials;
- corrupted database;
- Supabase outage;
- Render outage;
- region outage;
- storage deletion;
- ransomware/hostile mutation;
- wrong-project deployment.

For each incident define:

DETECTION
CONTAINMENT
BACKUP_SELECTED
RESTORE_TARGET
DNS/API CUTOVER
VALIDATION
ROLLBACK
OWNER_APPROVAL
COMMUNICATION

Store the runbook in the repository and Owner Recovery Center.

==================================================
13. OWNER DATA RECOVERY CENTER
==================================================

Build:

Admin HQ
→ Data Recovery

The owner must be able to view:

- PITR status;
- latest restorable time;
- backup health;
- daily snapshots;
- weekly backups;
- monthly archives;
- storage backup health;
- latest restore drill;
- current RPO/RTO;
- protected tables;
- blocked destructive operations;
- recovery alerts;
- incident history.

Owner actions:

- Create Snapshot
- Export Backup
- Run Validation
- Start Dry-Run Restore
- Compare Backup
- Restore Soft-Deleted Record
- Generate Recovery Report
- Open Disaster Runbook

Production restore must require:

- recent owner authentication;
- confirmation;
- reason;
- impact preview;
- backup ID;
- optional second approver;
- audit entry.

Do not allow one-click unreviewed production overwrite.

==================================================
14. DAILY EXECUTIVE RECOVERY REPORT
==================================================

Generate a daily report containing:

- PITR enabled/disabled
- latest restorable time
- daily backup status
- storage backup status
- checksum status
- row-count drift
- protected-table count
- blocked destructive operations
- orphan records
- restore drill status
- unresolved incidents
- RPO compliance
- RTO compliance

Send an alert only when action is required.

Store reports in:

- immutable audit storage;
- Admin HQ Recovery Center;
- owner notification history.

==================================================
15. MONITORING AND ALERTS
==================================================

Alert the owner for:

- PITR disabled;
- backup failure;
- storage-copy failure;
- unexpected row-count drop;
- destructive query blocked;
- large bulk update;
- RLS policy removed;
- backup retention changed;
- checksum mismatch;
- restore drill failure;
- database unavailable;
- storage bucket unavailable;
- unusual admin activity.

Every alert must include:

- timestamp
- service
- entity
- severity
- trace ID
- affected records
- automatic response
- owner action required

==================================================
16. FINANCIAL DATA PROTECTION
==================================================

For financial records:

- use append-only ledger design;
- never update historical ledger entries silently;
- corrections use compensating entries;
- require idempotency keys;
- reconcile wallet balances against ledger;
- record transaction hashes/checksums;
- use double-entry accounting where applicable;
- audit every payment/status change;
- require approval for high-value actions.

Verify:

TOTAL_WALLETS=
TOTAL_LEDGER_ENTRIES=
BALANCE_RECONCILIATION=
ORPHAN_TRANSACTIONS=
DUPLICATE_IDEMPOTENCY_KEYS=
MISMATCHES=

==================================================
17. ACCESS AND SEPARATION OF DUTIES
==================================================

Separate permissions for:

- normal application runtime;
- database administration;
- backup creation;
- backup deletion;
- restore execution;
- audit review;
- financial approval.

The production service role must not have permission to delete immutable
backups.

Rork and IVX IA must not be able to perform an unapproved production restore.

==================================================
18. SECURITY TESTS
==================================================

Run tests for:

- unauthorized backup access;
- unauthorized restore;
- unauthorized deletion;
- expired owner session;
- missing second approval;
- modified backup checksum;
- wrong-project backup;
- malicious migration;
- compromised runtime credential;
- mass deletion attempt.

Expected behavior:

- operation blocked;
- alert generated;
- audit event written;
- production remains unchanged.

==================================================
19. FINAL LIVE RECOVERY TEST
==================================================

Perform a controlled non-production recovery test:

1. create test member;
2. create test profile;
3. create test property/deal record;
4. create test transaction;
5. create test chat message;
6. upload test file;
7. create backup;
8. delete/alter test data only in isolated recovery environment;
9. restore from backup;
10. verify all records and file;
11. compare checksums;
12. measure RPO and RTO;
13. remove test data safely.

Do not perform destructive testing against production.

==================================================
20. FINAL EVIDENCE
==================================================

Return:

PITR

- enabled
- retention
- earliest restore
- latest restore

DATABASE BACKUPS

- daily status
- weekly status
- monthly status
- latest checksum
- latest tested restore

STORAGE BACKUPS

- buckets protected
- objects protected
- off-site copy
- versioning
- restore test

IMMUTABILITY

- backup lock
- deletion denial test
- separate role/account
- audit result

RECOVERY

- latest drill
- actual RPO
- actual RTO
- tables restored
- objects restored
- result

OWNER CENTER

- route
- Android result
- web result
- owner auth
- actions tested

MONITORING

- daily report
- alerts
- row-count monitoring
- PITR monitoring
- storage monitoring

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT
==================================================

Return exactly one:

IVX ENTERPRISE DATA PROTECTION VERIFIED

or

IVX ENTERPRISE DATA PROTECTION NOT CERTIFIED

Do not return VERIFIED unless:

- PITR is enabled or an equivalent continuous-recovery solution exists;
- daily encrypted backups run;
- off-site backups exist;
- storage objects are separately backed up;
- immutable copies exist;
- restore drills pass;
- RPO/RTO are measured;
- destructive actions are guarded;
- soft-delete works;
- owner Recovery Center works;
- daily reports and alerts work;
- financial records reconcile;
- evidence is complete.

If incomplete, return only:

FAILED_CONTROL:
CURRENT_RISK:
DATA_AT_RISK:
OWNER_ACTION_REQUIRED:
EXACT_SCREEN:
EXACT_ACTION:
AUTOMATIC_NEXT_STEP:
